zh.lifeSummary: 當Andrew James Cunningham於1660年7月19日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦劍橋大學默里 倫敦時，他的父親James Cunningham30歲，母親Marion Borthwick23歲。他於1694年與Elizabeth Nichols在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1726年10月10日在美國康乃狄克州劍橋大學默里 倫敦格洛托享年66歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Andrew James Cunningham于1660年7月19日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦劍橋大學默里 倫敦时，他的父亲James Cunningham30岁，母亲Marion Borthwick23岁。他于1694年与Elizabeth Nichols在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1726年10月10日在美国康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦格洛托享年66岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cunningham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the province of Cunningham in Ayrshire, first recorded in 1153 in the form Cunegan, a Celtic name of uncertain origin. The spellings in -ham, first recorded in 1180, and in -ynghame, first recorded in 1227, represent a gradual assimilation to the English placename element -ingham.

Irish: surname adopted from Gaelic Ó Cuinneagáin ‘descendant of Cuinneagán’, a personal name from a double diminutive of the Old Irish personal name Conn meaning ‘leader, chief’. This name is also adopted for Ó Connacháin, a variant of Ó Connagáin ‘descendant of Connagán’, from a diminutive of the personal name Conn.
History: A family of this name (see 1 above) can be traced back to Wernebald de Cunynghame, who was granted the manor of Cunningham by Hugh de Morville in the early 12th century.

variants.name: Cunigan
meanings: Altered form of Scottish or Irish   Cunningham  .

variants.name: Conahan
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Connachaín (see Cunningham ).

variants.name: Conaghan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Cormac, Fergal.
Scottish (Lanarkshire and Renfrewshire) Irish (Donegal): from Ó Connacháin, a variant of Ó Connagáin, see Cunningham .

variants.name: Cuningham
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Cunningham  .

variants.name: Cummingham
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Cunningham  .

variants.name: Cunnigham
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Donegal): variant of   Cunningham  .

nameMeanings.name: Andrew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the Greek name Andreas, a short form of any of various compound names derived from andr- ‘man, warrior’. In the New Testament this is the name of the first disciple to be called by Jesus. After the Resurrection, St Andrew preached in Asia Minor and Greece. He is traditionally believed to have been crucified at Patras in Achaia. He was one of the most popular saints of the Middle Ages and was adopted as the patron of Scotland, Russia, and Greece. It has long been among the most popular boys' names in the English-speaking world, especially in Scotland. Its popularity in England was further enhanced by its use as a British royal name for Prince Andrew ( b. 1960 ), the Duke of York.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.description: New London County was one of four original counties in Connecticut that were established on May 10, 1666

en.lifeSummary: When Andrew James Cunningham was born on 19 July 1660, in New London, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, his father, James Cunningham, was 30 and his mother, Marion Borthwick, was 23. He married Elizabeth Nichols in 1694, in Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 10 October 1726, in Groton, New London, Connecticut, United States, at the age of 66.

ja.lifeSummary: Andrew James Cunninghamが1660年7月19日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ロンドンニュー ロンドンで生まれた時，父James Cunninghamは30歳，母Marion Borthwickは23歳であった。1694年にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州でElizabeth Nicholsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1726年10月10日にアメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ニュー ロンドングロトンで66歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Andrew James Cunningham(이)가 1660년 7월 19일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던 뉴 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Cunningham(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Marion Borthwick(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1694년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주에서 Elizabeth Nichols(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1726년 10월 10일 미국 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던 그로턴에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.19 July 1660–10 October 1726: 19 de julho de 1660–10 de outubro de 1726

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Andrew James Cunningham nasceu em 19 de julho de 1660, em New London, New London, Conecticute, British Colonial America, seu pai, James Cunningham, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Marion Borthwick, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Nichols em 1694, em Conecticute, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 10 de outubro de 1726, em Groton, New London, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América, com 66 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Andrew James Cunningham родился 19 июля 1660 г. в местности ню лондон, ню лондон, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени James Cunningham было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Marion Borthwick, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Nichols в 1694 г. в местности Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 10 октября 1726 г. в местности гротон, ню лондон, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 66 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Andrew James Cunningham nació el 19 de julio de 1660, en New London, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, James Cunningham, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Marion Borthwick, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Nichols en 1694, en Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 10 de octubre de 1726, en Groton, New London, Connecticut, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 66 años.
